(1) These rules may be called the Central Motor Vehicles (1st Amendment) Rules, 2014. (2) Save as otherwise provided in these rules, they shall come into force on the 1st October 2014. 2. In the Central Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989 (hereinafter referred to as the principal rules), in rule 2, after clause (y), the following clause shall be inserted, namely:— ‘(z) “quadricycle” means a four wheeled vehicle and having the following features, namely:— (i) fully enclosed body structure with doors and having steering control through steering wheel with the top edge of the doors having at least 200 mm from adjacent seat base top surface; (ii) with sides and top not made of flexible material such as rexin, cloth, plastic etc.; (iii) for goods vehicle, cargo body internal area not less than 30% of the vehicle’s width multiplied by its length; (iv) maximum permissible kerb weight of 450 kg in case of passenger vehicle and 550 kg in case of goods vehicle; (v) maximum seating capacity of four (including driver) in case of passenger vehicle and two (including driver) in case of goods vehicle with independent seats provided for the driver and co- passenger in the front row in the case of both passenger vehicle and goods vehicle; (vi) maximum permissible pay load of 550 kg. in case of goods vehicles; (vii) maximum permissible propulsion power of 15 kW of the prime mover be 15 kW, when tested as per Chapter 1 of Part IV of MoSRTH/ TAP 115-116 as amended from time to time for Compression Ignition engines and battery operated vehicles and as per IS 14599-1999 for Spark Ignition engines; (viii) maximum design speed of 70 km per hour; and (ix) not having trailers or any provision of coupling a semi-trailer. -- 5 of 7 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] Explanation:—For the purpose of clause (iv), kerb weight does not include mass of batteries in the case of electric/ hybrid vehicles’. 3. In rule 50 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (1), in clause (vi) for the words “For two and three wheelers” the words “For two wheelers, three wheelers and quadricycles” shall be substituted. 4. In rule 93 of the principal rules,— (a) in sub-rule (1), the following proviso shall be inserted at the end and before the Explanation, namely:— “Provided that the overall width of a quadricycle shall not exceed 1.5metres.”; (b) in sub-rule (2), after clause (vii), the following clause shall be inserted, namely:— “(viii) in the case of quadricycle, 3 metres for passenger vehicle and 3.7 meter for goods vehicle”; (c) in sub-rule (4), after clause (iii), the following clause shall be inserted, namely:— “(iv) in the case of quadricycle, shall not exceed 2.5 metres”. 5. In rule 94 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (3), in clause (iv), for the words “two wheeler and three wheeler”, the words “two wheeler, three wheeler and quadricycle” shall be substituted. 6. In rule 95 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (1), in the first proviso, for the words “in the case of two and three wheelers” the words “in the case of two wheeler, three wheeler and quadricycle” shall be substituted. 7. In rule 96 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (4), in clause (ii), for the words “motor vehicles” the words “motor vehicles including quadricycles” shall be substituted; 8. In rule 98 of the principal rules, after sub-rule (3), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:— “(3A) On and after 1st October 2014, the steering effort of quadricycle shall conform to Indian Standard IS 11948-1999, as amended from time to time”. 9. In rule 101 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (2) after clause (ii), the following clause shall be inserted, namely:— “(iii) AIS 045/ 2004, in the case of quadricycles, on and after 1st October, 2014”. 10. In rule 115 of the principal rules,— (i) in sub-rule (2), in paragraph (i), in the Table, for the expression “2 & 3-wheelers” wherever they occur, the expression “Two-wheelers, three wheelers and quadricycles” shall be substituted. (ii) in sub-rule 14, in paragraph (F), – (a) in sub-paragraph (i), for “1st April, 2010”, the following shall be substituted, namely:— “1st April, 2010 and quadricycles manufactured on or after 1st October 2014.”; (b) in sub-paragraph (ii), for “1st October, 2010”, the following shall be substituted, namely:— “1st October, 2010 and quadricycles manufactured on or after 1st October 2014.”; (c) in sub-paragraph (iii),— (A) in the opening portion, for the words “two and three wheeler vehicles” the words “two wheelers, three wheelers and quadricycles” shall be substituted; (B) in the Table for “Three wheelers” at both the places where they occur, the words “Three wheelers and quadricycles” shall be substituted. 11. In rule 117 of the principal rules, after sub-rule (3), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:— “(4) Every quadricycle manufactured on and after 1st October 2014, shall be fitted with a speedometer conforming to the requirements of Indian Standard :11827-2008 specified by Bureau of Indian Standards”. 12. In rule 118 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (1), in the first proviso, for sub-clause (ii), by the following sub- clause, shall be substituted, namely:— “(ii) three wheelers and quadricycles” 13. In rule 119 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (1), after the proviso, the following proviso shall be inserted, namely:— “Provided further that on and after 1st October 2014, the requirements under this rule shall be applicable to every quadricycle”. -- 6 of 7 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 7 14. In rule 120 of the principal rules, in sub-rule (2), after the proviso the following proviso shall be inserted, namely: — “Provided further that in case of quadricycles, the permissible noise limits for vehicles in motion shall be 76 dB(A).”. 15. In rule 121 of the principal rules, after sub-rule (3), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:— “(4) The body of quadricycle shall prominently carry a label “Q” in capital and Arial font in black or any contrasting color in front and at the rear of vehicle and that the said label shall have suitable dimensions so that total display area is not less than 225 sq. cm.”. 16. (1) In rule 122, sub-rule (1), (a) in the proviso, for the letter and words “N category vehicle” the letter and words “N category vehicle and Goods Quadricycle” shall be substituted. (b) after the first proviso, the following proviso shall be inserted, namely:— “Provided further that on and after 1st October, 2014, the requirements under this rule shall be applicable to every quadricycle”. 17. In rule 125 of the principal rules, (a) in sub-rule (1), the following proviso shall be inserted, namely:— “Provided that on and after 1st October, 2014, the requirements under this rule shall be applicable to every quadricycle: Provided further that the requirement for safety belt assembly and safety belt anchorages for quadricycles shall conform to IS 15140: 2003 and IS 15139: 2002, respectively”; (b) in sub-rule (2), after the first proviso, the following proviso shall be inserted, namely:— “Provided further that on and after 1st October, 2014, the requirements under this rule shall be applicable to every quadricycle.”; (c) after sub-rule (6), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:— “(7) On and after 1st October, 2014, the seats, their anchorages and head restraints for quadricycles shall be in accordance with the specifications of vehicle Group B as specified in AIS 023: 2005 as amended from time to time till the corresponding BIS specifications are notified under the Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 1986 (63 of 1986).” . [F. No. RT-11028/09/2013-MVL] SANJAY BANDOPADHYAYA, Jt. Secy. Note:—The principal rules were notified in the Gazette of India vide G.S.R. 590(E), dated 2nd June, 1989 and last amended vide G.S.R. 664(E) dated 27th September, 2013.
